mount_note: Mount re-solved 2026-07-17 to SPIIDEO-FRAME ALIGNMENT (their calibration's up - pitch-plane-derived, NOT verified plumb; do not treat as gravity for physics) via dense reg-SIFT roll measurement against Spiideo's own Play render of game afb81f5f (30 matched framings; raw roll rms 7.82deg -> 1.31deg). The previous frame (TILT 48.272/YAW -2.585/ROLL -0.554, Kabsch to the old eyeballed frame) was ~10deg rotated vs theirs (8.6deg about x + 5.9deg about z) - views LEANED at panned framings, reading as corner curvature. CAVEATS (CV review 2026-07-17): residual has a tilt-monotone ~-0.10deg/deg structure the up-axis model can't express (instrument alias vs higher-order datum - unexplained); the YAW component (+4.4deg) is invisible to roll measurements and unvalidated; sampled domain pan -58..+67 / tilt -13..-36 only. PRE-FANOUT gates: plumb-line verticals on the corrected mesh, |pan|>90 samples, fov-perturbation aliasing check, second game, coverage re-check, and regenerate per-game aim-track + tracklets IN LOCKSTEP (they encode the old frame). Harness: PLAYHUB/scripts/follow-re/mount-roll/.
window_note: Scene window = Spiideo REAL Nazwa mesh ground truth (their calibration S3 bucket, fetched 2026-07-13 via stream.projectionParameters.variants — see AIM_RESUME): pan +/-122.75, ceiling +37.07, their floor -81.70. We keep floor -89.95 (below-camera view; only adds downward freedom, invisible at zoom-out). The capture boundary (frame-edge culling) shapes the visible scallop INSIDE this window, exactly as theirs.
